metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC3: add a deterministic proving command for the 'no side effects' claim — e.g. `strace -e trace=execve bash infra/scripts/compose-up.sh --print-volumes 2>&1 | grep -E '(dnf|systemctl|docker)' | diff - /dev/null` or a mock-path test that asserts those binaries are never exec'd.
findings.finding: AC3's 'no side effects' claim has no deterministic proving command: 'running it on a dev machine without docker privileges' does not prove that dnf, systemctl, or other side-effecting calls inside compose-up.sh are skipped — it only proves that docker commands fail gracefully (or silently) in that environment.
findings.suggested_fix: State the default proving command in AC3: `strace -e trace=execve bash infra/scripts/compose-up.sh --print-volumes 2>&1 | grep -cE '"(dnf|systemctl|docker)"' | grep -q '^0'`. If strace is unavailable, wrap the three binaries with stub scripts on PATH that `exit 1` and assert the overall command still exits 0.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan how `--print-volumes` enumerates volumes when compose-up.sh's internal mapping is restructured by e991 (vol:dir pairs, not uniform gerrit_${d}) — confirm the --print-volumes output contract is defined against e991's new loop structure, not the current broken one.
findings.finding: The plan asserts compose-up.sh's '--print-volumes' mode will print 'the exact external volume names it would create' as the single source of truth, but the existing provisioning loop ('for d in git index cache db etc logs plugins reviewbot; vol=gerrit_${d}') uses a uniform gerrit_${d} pattern that CANNOT express the gerrit_reviewbot_tickets volume (which maps dir 'reviewbot-tickets' to volume 'gerrit_reviewbot_tickets' — a hyphen/underscore split). The plan gives no specification of how --print-volumes will handle this non-uniform mapping, meaning the claim that '--print-volumes output IS the single source of truth' rests on an unspecified and currently broken mapping scheme.
findings.location: What section: 'compose-up.sh gains a side-effect-free --print-volumes mode: prints the exact external volume names it would create (one per line)' / infra/scripts/compose-up.sh lines 69-78
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: '--print-volumes iterates the same vol:dir pair map that e991 introduces (e.g., an associative array or explicit list of vol=name:dir=path entries), so its output is mechanically derived from the same data structure that drives docker volume create — not a separate hardcoded list.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC4: extend the self-test assertion (or add a dedicated grep) to verify the failure message contains 'compose-up.sh', e.g. `output=$(bash infra/scripts/config-check.sh 2>&1); echo "$output" | grep -q 'compose-up.sh'`.
findings.finding: AC4's requirement that the failure message 'points at compose-up.sh as the fix site' has no corresponding proving step — the self-test in AC1 only asserts exit-non-zero and that the volume name appears; it does not assert the message content about compose-up.sh.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC4; cross-reference AC1 self-test description
findings.suggested_fix: In the self-test block for check 5, add: `echo "$CHECK5_OUTPUT" | grep -q 'compose-up.sh' || { echo 'AC4 FAIL: fix-site pointer missing'; exit 1; }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that check 5 must validate `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` exits 0 before trusting its output, and emit a distinct, actionable error (not a volumes-missing error) if the subprocess fails — preventing silent false-negatives or confusing false-alarms from a broken --print-volumes implementation.
findings.finding: If `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` exits non-zero (script error, missing executable, unexpected early exit), config-check.sh will collect empty output and compare an empty provisioned-volumes set against the compose-declared volumes — silently reporting zero drift and passing CI with a false-green result, exactly the failure mode the gate is meant to prevent.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the check 5 spec: 'capture both stdout and exit code from compose-up.sh --print-volumes; if exit code != 0, fail immediately with message: "compose-up.sh --print-volumes failed (exit N) — cannot verify volume provisioning" and do NOT proceed to set comparison.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan HOW `--print-volumes` enumerates volume names without introducing a second copy — e.g., 'early-exit guard at top of script prints `${vol}` inside the same provisioning loop, then exits 0' — so the implementation is unambiguously the single-source design the plan claims.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `--print-volumes` will be the 'single source of truth — no second copy of the list to drift,' but does not specify the implementation mechanism. If `--print-volumes` enumerates volumes via a new separate list (e.g., a hardcoded array), that IS a second copy that can drift from the provisioning loops — directly contradicting the stated design goal. If it re-uses the provisioning loop, the plan should say so explicitly. The spec as written leaves the mechanism ambiguous, making it impossible to verify the 'no second copy' invariant without implementation details.
findings.location: What section — `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` gains `--print-volumes` mode; 'The provisioning list itself is the single source of truth — no second copy of the list to drift.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: '`--print-volumes` is implemented as an early-exit guard at the top of compose-up.sh: if `$1 == --print-volumes`, the script enters the provisioning loop, prints each `${vol}` name to stdout, then `exit 0` — before any dnf/systemctl/docker invocation. This ensures the printed list is derived from exactly the same loop that does the real provisioning, with no second enumeration.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that check 5 explicitly checks the exit code of `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` and emits a distinct hard-error ('provisioner script exited non-zero; cannot determine provisioned volumes') rather than treating empty output as 'nothing provisioned'.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how config-check.sh handles a non-zero exit from `compose-up.sh --print-volumes`. If the --print-volumes branch itself fails (set -euo pipefail fires on a bash error inside compose-up.sh), config-check.sh captures empty stdout and then either (a) silently treats the empty list as 'nothing provisioned' — which incorrectly reports every declared volume as missing rather than 'provisioner script failed' — or (b) propagates the non-zero exit as a distinct hard-error with a clear message. The plan implies behavior (a) (it says 'fail if any declared external volume is not provisioned'), which would produce a confusing, misleading failure message rather than a clear 'could not determine provisioned volumes' error. The plan should specify that the --print-volumes exit code is explicitly checked and produces a distinct 'provisioner script failed' hard-error path.
findings.suggested_fix: In the check 5 spec, add: 'If `compose-up.sh --print-volumes` exits non-zero, check 5 immediately fails with the message "config-check: FAIL — compose-up.sh --print-volumes exited <N>; cannot determine provisioned volumes" and does NOT proceed to the volume comparison.'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out path for check 5 (e.g., a documented `make config-check SKIP_CHECK5=1` escape hatch, or a revert procedure) so that a false-positive from the new gate does not require an emergency rollback of the whole change.
findings.finding: The plan changes the behavior of the existing `make config-check` target (a shared CI interface consumed by test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml) by adding a mandatory new check that will fail on any drift, but specifies no rollback/back-out mechanism if the new check emits a false positive or has a bug post-landing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line escape hatch such as `SKIP_DRIFT_GATE=1 make config-check` (guarded by an env-var check at the top of check 5) with a note that its use requires a tracking ticket, giving operators a break-glass option without full revert.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documented interface contract for `--print-volumes` in `compose-up.sh` (e.g., a prominent header comment or CLAUDE.md entry) stating that config-check.sh depends on this flag and its stdout format, so future maintainers cannot accidentally remove it without breaking CI visibly.
findings.finding: The `--print-volumes` flag is a new interface contract between config-check.sh and compose-up.sh, but the plan does not call for any documentation of this contract (e.g., in script headers, CLAUDE.md, or a design note), leaving future maintainers of compose-up.sh unaware that removing or renaming the flag silently breaks the CI gate.
findings.location: What section — 'compose-up.sh gains a side-effect-free --print-volumes mode'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to compose-up.sh near the --print-volumes implementation: '# CONTRACT: config-check.sh check 5 depends on --print-volumes printing one volume name per line to stdout and exiting 0. Do not remove or rename without updating infra/scripts/config-check.sh.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-time audit step (or pre-condition statement) confirming that all external volumes currently in docker-compose.yml are already provisioned by compose-up.sh *except* `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets`, so AC2's 'exits 0 after e991' claim is grounded.
findings.finding: The plan assumes `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` is the only unprovisioned external volume in docker-compose.yml (the one fixed by e991), but does not verify or acknowledge whether any *other* external volumes currently declared in docker-compose.yml are also missing from compose-up.sh. If additional drift exists beyond the e991 case, check 5 will fail on the stacked tree and AC2 cannot be met — yet the plan presents AC2 as straightforwardly satisfied once e991 lands.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Dependencies section, add: 'Pre-condition verified: as of the base commit, `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` is the only external volume in docker-compose.yml absent from compose-up.sh (confirmed by running the new check against the pre-e991 tree and observing exactly one failure).' If other gaps are found, list them and declare whether they are deferred or fixed in this stack.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `--print-volumes` is justified over the simpler alternative of having check 5 parse `compose-up.sh`'s source directly for `docker volume create` invocations (same python3 tooling already present), which avoids adding a CLI mode to a deploy orchestrator for a single call-site.
findings.finding: The `--print-volumes` CLI mode added to compose-up.sh has exactly one planned call-site (check 5 in config-check.sh), violating Rule-of-Three for new abstractions. It also adds argument-parsing/dispatch logic to a deploy orchestrator to serve a linting script, when config-check.sh could instead derive the provisioned-volume list by parsing compose-up.sh's own source text (using the same python3 it already uses for YAML) — achieving the same single-source-of-truth property without a new CLI contract on the deploy script.
findings.location: What section — '`infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` gains a side-effect-free `--print-volumes` mode'
findings.suggested_fix: In check 5, parse compose-up.sh's source directly: extract the volume names from `docker volume create ... "${vol}"` calls using python3/regex (or a small awk), compare against docker-compose.yml's external volumes. This gives the same single-source-of-truth guarantee with zero new CLI surface on compose-up.sh.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
